<!-- auto-generated by build_wiki.py -->
# st-fetch — Import external content into a container

Imports external content into the Cross pipeline for fact-checking, improvement, and publishing.
`st-prep` runs automatically after every successful fetch — use `--no-prep` to skip it and store
a raw data entry only.

**Pipeline position:** `st-fetch` → `st-prep` (auto) → `st-fact` / `st-fix` / `st-merge` / `st-post`

---

## Examples

```bash
# Import a local file (st-prep runs automatically)
st-fetch --file report.md report.json

# Fetch a web page — model is set to the domain (e.g. "bbc.co.uk")
st-fetch --url https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/article report.json

# Fetch an X (Twitter) post by numeric tweet ID
st-fetch 1234567890 post.json

# Paste from clipboard
st-fetch --clipboard notes.json

# Skip st-prep — store raw data entry only
st-fetch --file report.md report.json --no-prep

# Bypass fetch cache — always fetch live
st-fetch 1234567890 post.json --no-cache
```

---

## Source Types & make/model Values

Every container entry written by `st-fetch` records its origin using two fields:

| Source | `make` | `model` | Status |
|--------|--------|---------|--------|
| `--file` | `st-fetch` | `file` | ✅ Implemented |
| `--clipboard` | `st-fetch` | `clipboard` | ✅ Implemented |
| `--url <any URL>` | `st-fetch` | domain (e.g. `bbc.co.uk`) | ✅ Implemented |
| `<tweet_id>` (X/Twitter API) | `st-fetch` | `x.com` | ✅ Implemented |
| `--url <reddit URL>` | `st-fetch` | `reddit.com` | ✅ Via `--url` |
| `--pdf <file>` (PDF import) | `st-fetch` | `pdf` | 🔄 Future |
| `--rss <feed URL>` (RSS/Atom feed) | `st-fetch` | `rss` | 🔄 Future |
| `--youtube <url>` (transcript) | `st-fetch` | `youtube.com` | 🔄 Future |

`make` is always `"st-fetch"` — it identifies the tool. `model` identifies the source platform
or format. For `--url`, the model is automatically set to the domain of the URL (with `www.`
stripped), so every web source is naturally self-describing.

---

## Full Refresh Pipeline

```bash
st-fetch --file old_report.md old_report.json   # import (st-prep runs automatically)
st-bang old_report.json                          # regenerate with all 5 AI providers
st-cross old_report.json                         # cross-product fact-check
st-merge old_report.json                         # synthesize best version
st-post --site myblog old_report.json            # publish to Discourse
```

---

## Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| `--file PATH` | — | Import a local `.txt` or `.md` file |
| `--url URL` | — | Fetch a web page and extract visible text |
| `--clipboard` | — | Import text from the system clipboard |
| `--no-prep` | off | Skip `st-prep` — store raw data entry only |
| `--no-cache` | off | Bypass fetch cache; always fetch live (tweet_id source) |
| `-v` / `--verbose` | off | Verbose output |
| `-q` / `--quiet` | off | Minimal output |

**Requirements:** `X_COM_BEARER_TOKEN` in `.env` is required for `<tweet_id>` fetches only.

---

## For Developers

- `make` is always `"st-fetch"` for all entries created by this tool (defined as `AI_MAKE` in `ai_url.py`).
- `"st-fetch"` is **not** in `AI_HANDLER_REGISTRY` — `st-prep` detects this and reads content directly from `data["markdown"]` / `data["text"]` instead of calling `get_data_content()`.
- For `--url`, the model is derived from `urllib.parse.urlparse(url).netloc.lstrip("www.")` — no special-casing per domain needed.
- Twitter/X fetches require `X_COM_BEARER_TOKEN` in `.env`; responses are cached in `~/.cross_api_cache/` by MD5 of the request params.
